fields.description:
## Why
Deletion/asymmetric-change review is a positive analysis task the reviewer won't do unless prompted, but
it doesn't belong in the always-on base prompt. rebar's grounding oracle has no reverse-reference lookup,
so a purely deterministic version is imprecise new work. The aligned design is a specialist LLM overlay
gated by a deterministic diff-content trigger.

## What
- New capability: content-triggered overlays — a deterministic diff-content predicate that activates an
  overlay, alongside today's path-glob triggers (registry.glob_triggered_overlays seam).
- REGISTER the overlay id: add `deletion-impact` to the closed OVERLAY_IDS tuple in registry.py (grounded
  prerequisite — every dispatch path validates against OVERLAY_IDS and silently DROPS an unknown id, so a
  criteria_routing.json entry alone would never dispatch).
- deletion-impact overlay (agentic, findings-only, one-hop): triggered when the diff removes a
  def/class/signature (polyglot regex over '-' lines; the regex is expected to evolve per language).
  Caller-search mechanism (specified): the overlay uses its read-only tools (search_files / read_file;
  AST/LSP when available) to find surviving references to the removed/renamed symbol in UNCHANGED files
  and flags dangling ones. Advisory/coach-not-block; claims flow through Pass-2 verify + Pass-3 decide.
  Over-firing the trigger costs one advisory pass, never a wrong verdict.

## Scope
registry.py (trigger seam + OVERLAY_IDS), gates/code-review.yaml wiring,
reviewers/code-review-deletion-impact.md, criteria_routing.json entry (SHARED FILE — coordinate with
stories E and F which also edit it; file_impact declared so next_batch serializes them).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `deletion-impact` added to the OVERLAY_IDS closed tuple; dispatch reaches the overlay.
- [ ] Content-trigger seam added and unit-tested; path-glob triggers unaffected.
- [ ] Overlay fires on removed def/class/signature lines, not on unrelated diffs.
- [ ] Caller-search mechanism documented (tools, one-hop scope); dangling-deletion recall eval passes; a
      valid rename-with-callers-updated does not fire.
- [ ] Overlay is advisory (no new BLOCK source).
